data.narrative:
Dexterity, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company, is valued at $1.65B post-money following $291M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises five private robotics companies at identical stage and sector, with one public comparable (277810.KQ). The peer set exhibits wide dispersion: valuations range from $300M (GrayMatter Robotics) to $10.0B (1X Technologies), with a median of $500M.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $3.1B. Peer-median methodology anchors to the $500M median across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables. These methods produce a low estimate of $425M, median of $1.8B, and high of $3.6B.

The current $1.65B valuation aligns with the triangulated median of $1.8B, placing Dexterity near the center of the derived range. Key uncertainties stem from the substantial dispersion within the peer set—the $10.0B outlier (1X Technologies) versus the $300M floor—reflecting heterogeneous risk profiles, customer traction, and capital efficiency across robotics ventures. Resolution requires deeper diligence on Dexterity's differentiation relative to high-outlier and low-quartile peers, particularly on near-term revenue trajectory and manufacturing scalability.
